message:
test(abi): read _IO_2_1_* aliases via addr_of_mut to avoid static-ref UB

Tighten the io_2_1_aliases_resolve_to_native_stdio_storage test so
it no longer takes a reference to a `static mut` (a clippy /
compiler-warning surface that future rustc releases may promote to
an error).

crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/stdio_abi_test.rs:
- Replace the unsafe block of raw `assert_eq!(stdin, stdin_ptr)`
  comparisons (which implicitly reference the static muts) with
  explicit `std::ptr::addr_of_mut!(stdin).read()` loads into locals
  (`stdin_global`, `stdout_global`, `stderr_global`,
  `io_stdin`, `io_stdout`, `io_stderr`), then run the equality
  asserts against the locals outside the unsafe block.
- Semantics are unchanged: the test still pins the invariant that
  the three `_IO_2_1_*` alias pointers refer to the same NativeFile
  storage as the canonical `stdin` / `stdout` / `stderr` statics.
  The addr_of_mut indirection is the idiomatic way to read a
  `static mut` on the stable path without forming a reference to it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
